plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-211 RP06.P03.M06.S01-RP06.P04.M03.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-211 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP06.P03 API-surface tail (Synthetic Fixture Set, Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP06.P04 UI-surface foundation (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, Primary Implementation Slice head) as nine descriptor sections over 150 planned rows, with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-212 handoff, without opening UI runtime execution, build runtime dispatch, API handler execution, API response serving, DMS runtime dispatch, Hermes runtime receipts, review/approval route runtime, state writes, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, workflow persistence, object storage, OCR, search, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-211 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (150 units, RP06.P03.M06.S01-RP06.P04.M03.S08, risk C) closing the RP06.P03 API-surface tail and opening the RP06.P04 UI-surface foundation. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and all no-write/no-real-data/authority boundaries hold. Pack identity, unit count, deliverable distribution (impl 53/contract 21/security_audit 16/test 14/hermes_evidence 6/claude_review 10/ui 28/fixture 2=150), phase counts (P03:92, P04:58), and micro-title counts reconcile across the authoritative closeout-pack-plan.json (source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65...), registry.js pack binding, the dms-core contract header, the baseline, and the prompt. All 7 required public exports exist (service.js x2, validators.js x5) and are surfaced via index.js. validateDmsCoreCp211P03CloseoutP04UiFoundationDescriptor enforces per-row descriptor_only/no-runtime/customer-safe/no-raw-payload plus section-specific no-leak guards (API response payload excluded, unauthorized data omitted, permission/audit detail excluded, UI runtime and build runtime not executed, Hermes runtime receipts not emitted, Claude final-approval not claimed); a grep over packages/dms/src found no real IO (no fetch/fs/http/db) — only descriptor attestation flags. Authority boundary is preserved: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required=true, and the Hermes packet is descriptor-only. command-evidence.json declares production_ready=false with status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout, correctly deferring enterprise-trust to human final approval. Review-evidence hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, valid JSON, ok:true), readiness=ready, prompt within size limit, forbidden tools absent, review schema present, read-tool inspection mode; no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, or missing fields. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. Note: human final approval remains the separate, required gate for any enterprise-trust/runtime-opening claim — this read-only review does not constitute final approval.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-211 dms core p03 closeout p04 ui foundation descriptor pack
